titleStmt.title: The Secretary of the United States Mission in Bulgaria ( Rewinkel ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Secretary of the United States Mission in Bulgaria ( Rewinkel ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 553. General Crane has just received from Turkish MinisterVasfi Mentes. copy of FonOff note to him dated August 31 in which question of neutral merchandise in transit through Bulgaria at time of armistice in 1944 is discussed. Such merchandise, a large quantity of which was Turkish property in transit from Switzerland, was blocked by ACC (Allied Control Council) order until neutral ownership could be established. FonOff informed Turks that it had vainly tried to secure from ACC (Allied Control Council) release of such goods and that now it had received a notification dated July 10, and signed by Chairman of ACC (Allied Control Council), to effect that all such blocked transit merchandise in customs and railroad warehouses must be delivered to booty section of the Bed Army for disposition by ACC (Allied Control Council). Notification further informed FonOff that this step was taken because changed circumstances had made it impossible to determine exactly ownership of such goods and question of payment therefor. FonOff stated that pursuant to these ACC (Allied Control Council) orders it was forced to seize all neutral merchandise caught in transit through Bulgaria at time of armistice.

Generals Crane and Oxley had no prior knowledge of this situation nor were they consulted concerning order issued by General Biryusov in name of ACC (Allied Control Council). They learned of it from the appeal to them, as members of ACC (Allied Control Council) by Turkish Minister. They will insist on discussion of subject in September 25 ACC (Allied Control Council) meeting.
Please inform JCS.Joint Chiefs of Staff.
